Y es que el hotel está LLENO de wallabies (canguros pequeños) pero lleno y pájaros que campan a sus anchas por el comedor. Ello hace que todo el jardín del hotel (incluso los pasillos del hotel) están llenos de caca de canguro que nadie limpia. La relación calidad precio es totalmente desproporcionada. Recomendamos buscar otras opciones para alojarse en las whitsundays.

Fecha del comentario: Fecha del comentario: 5 de abril de 2026
Me gusto:
We loved the pool area where the restaurants are. There were multiple pools and a kids pool. They were clean. One day it rained and was windy but staff came out and cleaned up the sticks and leaves in the pool. We also loved our room view and the enterance to the island. We were given complimentary drinks upon arrival which was nice.

Our room was nice and clean and house keeping did a good job. They just forgot to take some glassess and cups. 
The bathroom was nice although our fan didn't work and apparently it was a silent fan and if you put a paper next to it it'll still suck in air but it wouldn't. We did enjoy the ping pong table but i think more can be added into that area for night life as theres not much to do night time on the island..
No me gusto:
When we first got there at reception, the girl greeted us with bare minimum smile. She was so flat and I read previous reviews and expected it, i didn't let it get to me. The other staff made up for it though. Most were lovely, especially at Salti.
The amount we spent on food at the restaurants and the lack of food after 9pm. We had a toddler and she would get hungry throughout the night if we stayed up and wanted actual food. There is no small cafe or anything open. One night we were late for dinner reservation and couldn't get a table at Salti. We went upstairs to Infinity but their menu was confusing to understand no photos and barely had anything a toddler would eat. I don't think i ate that night.
We paid $1700 for our room and i only realised after that we had NO kitchen. We did pack a few packs of croissants and snacks which I was happy we did because of the obvious no kitchen and no option for food after hours. Surely people get hungry after 8?
We spent $700 for 5 days just on food lunch and dinner. Thank god children got to eat for free if we dined in. I understand its an island but once the restaurants close you literally have no access to any food or drinks until the morning.
Breakfast was the same everyday. We also had to buy our proper coffees (lattes, caps). I did not want the watery type provided.
There wasn't much activities for kids to do. The kids playground could be upgraded to have more features. Just had slide and swing. Kids club is $55 per kid which is really expensive so we didn't even bother -- a kids play area indoors in the atrium would be good as kids would run around in the area and would probably get annoying for some adults who are there on their own or as couples. There also could be more night activities or things for night time in the atrium. We had a singer on for a few nights which brought people together to mingle but on the last 2 nights she wasn't there and the whole place was also empty.

Titulo del comentario: The Living Reef experience at the hotel compansates for the low level of other services at the hotel
Tipo de Habitacion: Habitación Resort con 2 camas individuales
Fecha del comentario: Fecha del comentario: 13 de enero de 2026
Me gusto: The unique characteristic of this hotel is the Living Reef which offers several activities for kids and adults to get acquainted with the sea life at the Great Barrier Reef, which are not offered in any other facility on the wider area. The staff of the Living Reef Experience Center is well trained, friendly and professional. Especially snorkelling in the coral lagoon that is located around the central building of the resort was an amazing experience.
No me gusto:
The standard of cleanliness in both the guest rooms and the breakfast areas was insufficient. During our five-night stay, the housekeeping service was limited only to making the beds and replacing the towels. Furthermore, in the breakfast area, we noticed on several occasions that the crockery and silverware had not been properly washed.

Due to the schedule of the ferry transportation, it is not possible to schedule dinner anywhere else but the restaurants at the hotel. Access to other areas in the Whitsundays depends highly on the ferry transportation schedule, and is also quite costly. There are three dining options at the hotel which are expensive and the service level is not sufficient. Breakfast at the hotel offers several buffet options but it is not very tasty.

It was evident that the hotel is primarily staffed by young individuals with minimal professional experience. The constant daily rotation of personnel between various positions prevents them from gaining sufficient expertise in any specific area, leading to inadequate service across the board.

Titulo del comentario: You get what you pay for

Fecha del comentario: Fecha del comentario: 12 de enero de 2026
Me gusto:
We got the Vista suite, their most expensive room, and for the price and compared to what yould get for that price in the other whitsundays islands with hotels, i think daydream offers a lot of value for the money you pay, at least in terms of the room WE got, the vista suite was excellent, newly renovated, beautiful views, almost perfect besides their inexplicably being no minibar or even 1 bottle of water in a huge appartment room which had a fully kitted out kitchen (were are you meant to buy the food to cook there though?? Youll be hard pressed in daydream island to even find a can of pringles and a coke zero)
The aquarium at the property is very impressive and well maintained, specially the underwater coral reef viewing room which just seems like they spent half the budget for the entire island on building, the result is a very impressive, if somewhat out of place, experience.
No me gusto:
The saying is you get what you paid for, and with daydream that is certainly true, its a "cheap" whitsundays island, and as a result, the property feels run down in places (however I have to say their lobby area beats the much more expensive lobby at Hayman Island), the main issue during our stay was food, the restaurant food (we went to infinity) is just, not good, at all, it was so bad in fact that we opted just not to eat at all except for pringles and kitkats on our second night (we had to stay a 2nd night due to a cyclone cancelling all ferries, I have to say, daydream acted very well by offering us a 50% discount for the extra night) 

If they improved their food offerings and had a 24 hour shop to get basic snacks and drinks I think this place would be rated much much higher, however as It stands i can only recommend it for a 1 night stay.

Me gusto: The superior ocean view king room we stayed in for 5 nights was on the top floor (level 3) of the harbour wing with a spectacular view across the marina out towards south molle island. It only had a narrow balcony with no outdoor furniture but the weather was warm so we didn't mind having the doors closed, a/c on and enjoying the view from inside on the couch. The room was spacious, comfortable and quiet. There was no sheer curtain which could have been good to reduce the glare. It was a short walk to the main building where breakfast was served next to the living reef. We spent a lot of time enjoying the pools, living reef - including daily talks and tours, underwater observatory, lovers cove, snorkelling (with free hire of stinger suit as it was Oct 22nd stinger season had just started), kyaking, walking through the rainforest, reef exploration centre, coral restoration centre, walking to the pool at the southern end of the island, and laying by the pool. We enjoyed the mocktails, take away pizzas and veg burgers from salti. The iced chocolate from tonic and the cafe were also good. The gift shop had a range of souvenirs as well as snacks, pies, sandwiches and 1L milk. The cafe also had sandwiches and coffee but not open for long each morning. All of the staff were fantastic and made it so much more enjoyable. They must be well trained and have good managers. The welcome drink and shell necklace on arrival was a lovely first impression. The weather was perfect while we were there. We didn't do any paid activities but would like to do a glass bottom boat trip to the reef at south molle in future. Five nights was a great length of stay. It was very relaxing and we hope to return.
No me gusto: We had a fantastic time but there were things that could be improved. The main area we found disappointing was the food as vegetarian options were very limited. The prices were also quite high but it was more so the lack of options that was disappointing. The food from the upstairs restaurant Infinity was great but they didn't do take away and weren't open for lunch. Salti, downstairs, did take away pizzas which were good but the veg burger, which was nice, was only available for lunch. The gnocchi for dinner wasn't great. Some parts of the buffet breakfast were also disappointing eg there were no hash browns (just herbed roast potatoes or potato gems), mushrooms only occasionally, overseasoned grilled tomatoes, juice kept running out, glasses kept running out, no decaf coffee so had to buy from the cafe. It would've been good for the gift shop to also sell 1L soy milk and lactose free milk for us to use in our room for tea and coffee. The curlews and cockys were annoying at times. The snorkelling in lovers cove wasn't amazing but was still worthwhile, it also varied depending on the tide. They're doing a great job restoring the reef. All these aspects we mention would not stop us from returning and hopefully are not off putting for anyone else!

We weren’t told where to go after arrival, we waited an hour in the arrivals lounge before overhearing direct instructions to latest arriving guests. 
The resort is dated, visually it is dirty, mouldy and needs a lot of love. Stains on outdoor furniture on balconies, again lots of cleaning required even in the hallways to the rooms you can’t help but see it everywhere. 
Rooms are very noisy, can hear neighbours and hallway noise - doors etc from very early in the morning. 
STAFF! Lack of staff at all restaurants and bars. 
No pool towels in our room, next day cleaners removed them and they weren’t replaced so two trips to front desk for towels. 
Not one staff member stands out, not one had even the friendliest smile or stand out customer service which I found odd considering location and the fact that their customers are on holidays = best mood ever! 
Rooms were overpriced, everything overpriced $5 for can of soft drink, $15 RTD & $22 cocktails is insanely expensive for the lack of cleanliness and staff. 
Breakfast Buffet very basic, nothing stands out. 
Restaurant menus need a redo, very basic, not a lot of options however can say at multiple restaurants food was served promptly.

Me gusto:
If you have children then daydream island is a great holiday spot. 
The 10:00am reef talk educating on the marine life was really educational and enjoyable.
No me gusto:
I booked an ocean view room but ended up with a poolside view instead. I understand the island was really busy, but I feel some sort of compensation should have been offered, especially since the room also wasn’t ready until after 2pm.

It was also really confusing trying to find the room, more signage around the property would make a big difference.

For dinner, having to book a table for just 2 people feels unnecessary. The restaurants had plenty of space (both inside and outside), yet we almost weren’t seated when we walked in at 5:30pm because of a later booking at 7:30pm. We finished our whole meal by 6:30pm, and on both nights I noticed a lot of empty tables. Couples should be able to walk in without issues when there’s clearly room.

Don’t know why spa treatments are closed on a Sunday at big hotel. Very disappointing

An adults-only pool would also be a great addition since there are already multiple pools.

The wildlife is great but the balcony’s are so dirty with poo that’s not being cleaned or hosed down. 

breakfast was fine but pretty basic. the pastries in particular could definitely be better.

Me gusto:
We arrived off a late ferry and first impressions were of the long concrete corridors and massive atrium being a bit "institutional" but the room didn't feel that way. The room was spacious and comfortable. We liked watching the live feed from the lagoon on our TV! and being able to have takeaway Pizza and chips.  As we explored the next day, we were very impressed by the grounds and pool area. Amazing buffet breakfast with continental and cooked options. The underwater observatory was awesome and so relaxing.   We loved the Kangaroos, happily performing their "lawnmowing" duties and enjoyed the educational talk at the lagoon. Watching the migrating whales from the restaurant made up for the gull invasion ! The incorporation of so much wildlife really makes Daydream Island special.
We were lucky to see Helen the rock python on our walk around the island and enjoyed the challenge of the bush track.
We enjoyed a G&T at Lover's cove while watching a stunning sunset and listening to some great live music. 
Hats off to the gardening and maintenance staff as the resort is so well looked after. 
Our brief stay was too short and I would recommend a 3 to 4 night stay to have time to relax as well as try out the activities.
All the staff we encountered were friendly and happy to help. This would be the perfect place for a multi-generational holiday. Younger kids in particular would love it.
No me gusto:
Limited eating options on Sunday evening. Buffet dinner was good value if you were hungry but expensive if a small meal preferred.
 Some activities were not available on a Sunday, such as the glass bottom Kayaks.
Poor communications. Prior to our trip, I tried to telephone twice, with limited success, and wasn't phoned back as promised.

Beautiful views on the cross island bush walk. 2 nights was enough to see/do everything but if your idea of a holiday is just lying by the pool everyday,  you could be happy here for longer.
No me gusto:
It's all just so tired and screaming out for some loving including the staff. The gurney needs to be running 24/7 cleaning balcony's,  chairs,  external walk ways of dirty and mildew.
Everywhere you look there's something broken and in need of repair. Our room had broken curtains, dripping taps, mildew smelling wardrobe and a grotty balcony. Me gusto:
From the moment we arrived, the staff made our stay special. Everyone was incredibly friendly, helpful, and genuinely nice to deal with, making the experience feel warm and welcoming.

The resort itself was beautifully maintained gardens looked pristine, and every area felt clean and cared for. The pool area was stunning, a perfect spot to relax and soak up the island vibes.

Overall, the combination of attentive staff, immaculate grounds, and the gorgeous pool made our stay very enjoyable.
No me gusto:
Some parts of the resort feel dated and could use a refresh. The bed and pillows were uncomfortable, which made it hard to fully relax. Dining options were limited, with only two restaurants available. For a resort of this scale, it would be much better to have:
	•	A relaxed pub-style restaurant downstairs for casual meals and comfort food.
	•	An upmarket, modern Australian restaurant upstairs with elegant, creative dishes, more seafood, and a classy island vibe.

Being on an island, seafood should really shine on the menu.

A small touch that would elevate the guest experience would be QR codes to order cocktails by the pool, creating more convenience and that holiday feeling of being looked after without having to leave your lounge chair.

Me gusto:
The island is beautiful with an amazing wildlife. Pool area is nice however pools are not heated (cooler than the ocean), the whole facility looks nice from a distance. Breakfast was okay(ish), but barista coffee is only at the bars, not included in the breakfast. The entire resort has a huge potential to be a real 4-star resort but currently no maintenance, and lack of care. 
Staff are nice, but clearly no management directions are in place, the resort is running its own way.
No me gusto:
The resort needs a massive deep clean. Everything is dirty and mouldy even the cups at the breakfast. 
Mattress is very soft and towels are very worn. Sofa in the room is stained and bathroom is filthy. Shower screen is covered by limescale, silicone part are mouldy, room floor is sticky. 

The breakfast area accommodates the dish cleaning facility which is a terrible thing to watch while you have breakfast. 
Restaurants: Salti is below average and expensive with small portions (ok, pizzas are ok), Infinity is nice and yumm. You must book a table for every night otherwise take away is the only option..
There’s a small shop in the main building, mainly for souvenirs but water, soft drink and snack are available, but limited choice. Expensive too. 
Pool bar: extremely dirty (filthy!), cocktails are served in plastic ‘glass’ which turned yellow - was about to ask the guy to give my cocktail in a takeaway coffee cup. 

The price reflects the 4-star hotel’s however the quality only enough for weak 3-star hotel… 

Next time -> no next time, or when it will be cleaned properly.

Me gusto: Australians need to stake stock of what is in their own backyard and Daydream island is part of the glorious Whitsundays. A mere 2 hour flight to Hamilton island from Sydney and a 30 minute ferry ride lands you in paradise. Daydream was destroyed by cyclone Debbie in 2018. It has been rebuilt and refurbished. Our room an ocean view suite was very comfortable and the bed was great as were the products. There are a choice of very well priced restaurants and great beaches and pools. The island is a wildlife sanctuary with water birds, cockatoos and wallabies and kangaroos. The island is committed to restoration and preservation of the reef and has a great living lagoon which you can snorkel in at a reasonable fee. There are great excursions to Whitsunday island and famous Whitehaven beach with snorkelling trips to nearby reefs. Lovers Cove sunset drinks were great to watch the sunset and there was a great musician covering chilled tunes. Great place for both adults and kids and the staff were great special shout out to Evie and Jordan and Clorinda!

Me gusto: The island certainly has some magic to it. The reef sharks and observatory was my favourite. The surprise friendly kangeroos / wallabies (really not sure as there’s no staff really to tell you much) and cockatoos that visit the balcony was an added welcome we didn’t expect! The food at the Asian restaurant was very good, does break the bank a little but the quality was great. The restaurant staff (although they are all new and don’t know the menu) are all very nice. The reception staff were also nice. Watching fishies while eating breakfast was fun. The amenities in the room were great quality.
No me gusto:
Sadly the activities staff put a downer on the whole trip, notably ‘lazy Ryan’ at the kayaks kiosk and a young man behind the tour desk near reception were particularly very poor at their jobs. We were quite surprised at their utter lack of professionalism and helpfulness, they just want to get in, get paid to do nothing and get out. The guy selling tours seems like he wants to do anything other than sell tours, the whole time he spoke to us each day we visited the desk in the hopes to spend our money on amazing trips he would not even stand up from his desk and kept his eyes glued to his screen hoping we would go away. He continued to tell us there were no tours available so we called one of them ourselves from the number on their brochure and booked with them directly instead - who were lovely and saddened their tours are not being sold! I can’t remember the same but it was a yellow RIB boat to go snorkeling & to Whitehaven - highly
Recommend. Ryan at the kayaks boasts a similar attitude lounging back on his chair picking at his nails while watching his female colleague drag heavy kayaks up on the beach herself, we got there at exactly 4:01pm and were told we could not grab a kayak, when asking him what time is last call he said 4pm… the times of the free activities advertised are 9:30-4pm, too late and too early for those like us embarking on any day trips. There’s not much to do if you’re early birds like us as nothing opens until 9:30am / 10am - breakfast only. Also - the $24 cocktails are sadly not worth it. We’d be happy to pay a high price if they were good but they are very basic and placed in blenders. While the Asian restaurant was great and we went there twice, you’d really be quite bored and broke over the food options on the island. I’d say better management and more food options are gravely needed, we went in low season and had to book in advance so I can’t imagine high season.

Me gusto: Absolutely stunning location! Beautiful scenery and was nice to be greeted with a welcome drink and shell necklace on arrival. View from our room was incredible overlooking the marina. Awesome pools and was nice to have activities spread out over the island. Pool bar was great, heaps of room to lounge about around the pools too. Beds were comfy and was nice to have a bottle of sparkles in the fridge. Great movie selection too. Staff were mostly friendly and welcoming. Infinity restaurant was delicious and such a great view. Buffet breakfast was so good! Tonic bar was great and a special shout out to one of the bartenders Thomas. So lovely, great service and made a great cocktail! Nightly music here was also great. Great that kayaks, paddle boards, tennis, pool tables and ping pong were included. Rainforest walk was awesome and so many friendly wallabies around. Cockatoos came to our balcony every day. Great that ferry and airport transfers were included in the stay.
No me gusto:
Room cleanliness could have been better, mould around one of the doors, the bath needed a good scrub and re- seal and the floors needed a better vacuum. The beds need to be put on stoppers as they roll around everywhere. We were also surprised that there was no bottles water or cold water in the fridge. 
Most of the food was quite overpriced. Snorkelling at lovers cove was very average, nothing to see and the ocean on that side was way to rough. Would be better to make a spot around the marina, way calmer water and heaps of fish. Pool areas needed extra shading, some big umbrellas or something over the pool water. Activities not included in island were quite over priced. $295 for one jet ski for an hour is so much.

No me gusto: The cleanliness of the room and communal areas need work. Dirty skirtings and bed frame. Bathrooms dirty and empty shower gels . The cleaner broke a cup in our room and left it smashed all over the floor along with a pile of water where she had "mopped" . when i asked her to clean the shards up she replied that she had and hadnt left it- i wouldnt know about it if she hadnt left it. . We asked several times for extras in the room (towels, cups, toilet roll ) as the room was set up for 2 not 4 and only 2 beds , no sofa or chair as described . The first night it was dark and I went to ask about whether we had a sea view ( couldn't see it at night and assumed we would have a balcony being a superior sea view ) . I was only asking if it was a sea view and I felt the managers attitude was off. I appreicate that the next day we clearly had a sea view but i felt he could have been a little more polite than he was. Having said that he was pleasant on check in. Me gusto:
I like that is very calm and isolated place - you can enjoy the island and chill. There are a lot of activities you can do during your stay and the staff are very caring to your well being.
I loved that we got a very warm welcome and directions when we arrived at the resort.
Infinity Restaurant was delicious.
No me gusto:
The breakfast was good, but for me the only problem is the diversity of the breakfast. I would say on the last day I started to get bored with the breakfast because it is always the same everyday. Most of resorts would change the breakfast every day or every 2 days so people don't get bored with it.

The staff were really caring and very nice to talk to. I would say that, for the price (value for money) I would have expected an elevated experience compared to what I did. E.g. the barefoot bar, was okay, but needed more staff to cope with the amount of people waiting to order drinks - and there were not a big variaty of drinks. Also near the pool, would have been insteresting a QR code to order drinks and a waiter or waitress deliver the drink for you at the pool - this is what I mean by value for money.

Another thing is - some of the activities were only possible to do on specific days, which is fine - but I think the variaty of ativities could be increased so guests are able to do more of the activities if they want and split the queue for those. E.g. There are only one activity per time slot - instead of for example, 2 different activities at the same time. This would give guests more options to do things and potentially enjoy the resort even more with more options. I couldn't do the Sailing activity, as there were only 2 boats, and many people to do it (and classes were only on weekends).

Last but not least is the location - while it is in a very seclude location where you can enjoy the peaceful island, it is hard to get to the region activities (you need to keep booking the ferry to the continent or to Hamilton Island) to go to the external activities, and there are limited time slots that the ferry goes to the island - so you need to keep in mind the extra cost of the ferry and the limited time slots to go back and from the island.

Me gusto:
The whole island is beautiful. Very family friendly, particularly ‘kids eat free’ 12 and under. Great pools , also liked the opportunity to hire free equipment. We spent a great morning snorkelling off Lover’s Cove beach.  I really respect what the resort is doing in restoring the coral reefs, which were ruined after cyclone Debbie in 2016. I also recommend doing the living reef snorkelling tour with a marine biologist. The living reef was one of the highlights. 
The other side of the island is not as populated; it’s really beautiful around here and I recommend hanging out here. Nice beach and kayaks available. 
Also enjoyed the rainforest walk. 
Breakfast buffet was good. I think the pastries are made there, and there’s a fantastic cinnamon scroll. I thought Salti restaurant had great food, including pizza. I also appreciated the food from ‘the shack’, which is more casual, and by the pool for lunch. Sometimes they have a dinner buffet at Graze (where the breakfast buffet is); I highly recommend this. 
The atrium area (where reception is) is a beautiful space; really enjoyed this area and also having a drink in tonic bar. 
We also loved the wallabies roaming freely, not to mention the curlews stalking around like they own the place!
No me gusto:
The biggest thing for my son and I, is that we booked a twin room, however they assumed we were 2 (coupled) adults so gave us a king room. I’m not sure how they overbooked their twin rooms, but apparently this was the issue. I managed to get this changed to a twin room after a few nights, but my feedback to the resort would be- check first before you change someone’s booking. 
Otherwise, really just small things. There are small ways things could be improved. When staying, you’re a captured market and beholden to their restaurants, so I was conscious that some things seemed a bit pricey(although supply to the island is probably pricey). Some of the food a bit hit and miss at Graze for a la carte dinner. Also, restaurants have a habit of ‘dumbing down’ their kids menu, so my son ended up eating from adult menu a lot of the time (hence not eating for free). 
More non alcoholic options for kids ie smoothies would be awesome. Include an espresso for free  for breakfast- don’t make us pay $5 for a small coffee! You could use a voucher system for this. One coffee per breakfast. 
Also- and this is being REALLY picky- add some things to the breakfast buffet: I’m going to say hot chocolate, fresh waffles, more fruit…..

Titulo del comentario: Still a must try experience and great value for money.

Me gusto: The pools were great and the staff were fantastic. We stayed for two weeks and numerous staff went out of their way to help us with any extra issues or needs we had. The physical environment around the resort was beautiful and the resort itself had a great atmosphere.
No me gusto: The food was very hit and miss. My daughter’s dinner at Infinity the first night was disgusting (beef wragu) and inedible. I would have sent it back but think I was just in shock! I’m not talking just unappealing, I mean like I had just pulled it out of compost and scraped maggots off. Vile. It put us off ever eating there again. Food at Salti was also mostly not up to scratch. Pizza we tried once was not properly cooked with the crust still soft dough inside. Pizza we then tried at Graz’n was the same, plus a gooey mass of cheese sliding around on the top. We had to go into Airlie to buy groceries that could sustain us with only the use of a kettle. No toaster or microwave even. I ate two-minute noodles for the first time in decades rather than eat at the restaurants. When we’d run out of them I stuck to vegetable dishes like the roast pumpkin and tempura cauliflower (both pretty tasteless but at least edible). The only positives food-wise were the lemon hommus and roast carrots from Salti, as well as the hot chips, and the cocktails, especially the Tim Tam chocolate one, from the Barefoot Bar and Tonic Bar. The desserts were also excellent but very over-priced.

Me gusto:
Location was great - only 30 mins from HTI with plenty of ferries per day. 

Breakfast is included for ALL guests by default, so it is not a special deal from Booking.com, but a great (free) inclusion anyway. Lots of options, including GF and veg. 

The on-site reef and sea life is an amazing touch and a good business decision, seeing as the nearby Lover's Cove beach is basically dead. Barely saw any fish the 5 days we were there and coral are all dead due to the hurricane in 2017. 
Plenty of activities throughout the day for kids, but not nearly enough for adults. 

Under the ground aquarium view is amazing! 

Trivia and bingo were fun and you can win free drink vouchers too! 

They do the best they can on rainy days to provide more activities indoor and try to keep the kids entertained. 

Food is pricey, but reasonable portion sizes accommodate for it. 

Plenty of wildlife on the island, including wallabies, snakes, cockatoos, etc. 

To save costs and from an environmental conservation perspective, they offer a $10 room credit per day that you don't avail room service, which I thought was great!
No me gusto:
The staff were by far the worst part of the resort. Most of the staff are working holiday visa workers under the age of 25 and could not care about being there at all. The service is extremely inconsistent and mostly just disappointing. The whole time I was there, I think there were only 2 girls that were happy, positive and helpful. 

Organised a surprise to be left in our room for our anniversary, which we confirmed again upon arrival and they said it was already there, but it wasn't. Eventually some kid came up and literally handed up a bottle of champagne. 

No regards to safety - there are fully dark walking areas at night, including near swimming pools. A woman got hurt and the resort couldn't care less. Next day, someone cut themselves in the busiest area of the resort and no one was available to provide first aid, so guests assisted. Electrical tags are expired in the rooms. 

Air conditioner swing doesn't work, so you have to move it with your hands. Also, needed a service. Not only in our room, but most rooms that we visited. 

Were serving alcohol at Lover's Cove, which we bought and I asked for some water, but they said we have to buy it, which I believe is not the law. Free drinking water has to be provided near a place where alcohol is served. I didn't want to raise it with the waitress, so went to the reception to provide feedback and started by saying "I know you can't fix this now, but hoping you can look into it," to which the Duty Manager said "he didn't want to argue with me on this" and offered me 2 free bottles of water instead, which was just dismissive. 

External food and drinks not allowed on the island, but plenty of people brought them anyway, especially if you're travelling with kids, you always need snacks!

Tipo de Habitacion: Alojamiento Superior con cama extragrande y vistas al océano
Fecha del comentario: Fecha del comentario: 22 de junio de 2024
Me gusto: Wildlife all over the island was a pleasant surprise. Island was just big enough. Great buffet breakfast, large rooms, all activities included in the price. I would go back again. The food at the restaurants was exceptional. I haven't been impressed by a meal for a a long time. Every dish we ordered was superb. Then chef surely knowns what he is doing. Drink and food prices were not exorbarant for a remote Island, you would pay more at some city restaurants and bars. We went just before school holidays during the weekdays so there were no crowds and not many children. PS, the beaches are chunks of broken coral so wear some aqua shoes.
No me gusto:
Although most of the staff were pleasant at the resort there were too many times that I was greeted with a frown.
Being at a tropical island paradise it felt odd, almost like you were not welcomed sometimes.
For whatever reason this was sub 3 star guest service. 
Part of every great holiday is how the staff dynamics are, and sorry to say I have seen a lot better in less fancier establishments and compared to similar priced accommodation, guest service at other establishments have been much better in my experience. 
On 4 occasions over 2 days, 3-4 staff members would be standing around chatting and you found yourself waiting for service. I couldn't understand it.
A praise to the garden staff, they were a delight to talk to every time, they really seemed to enjoy being there. 
I didn't express my views in the rating as I could not put the blame on all the staff for a few bad apples. The Island is still fabulous.

Titulo del comentario: Good value for money, but not a place for a foodie.

No me gusto: The food on offer became quite monotonous very quickly. There are only 2 restaurants in this resort and after 5 days, as a group of 4, we were happy to leave the island as between us we had tasted most of the menu. The quality of the dinner food was good, but nothing exceptional. The only lunch menu is available in Salti's, and it is a tailored down version of the dinner menu, so the variety of food on offer is quite limited. For breakfast, it is the same buffet style everyday. The cleanliness in the breakfast area needs to be reviewed as I often got dirty plates and noticed some rust and general lack of cleanliness around the buffet area.

Me gusto: Location, living reef, smirking in the aquarium, snorkelling in the sea
No me gusto:
Not enough gluten free options. One is completely dependent on the island to provide food. My 13 year old son has Coeliac disease. There are very limited gluten free options available at the restaurants, poor choices for desert that is gluten free and most of the options for mains where seafood. 
Not enough knowledge about gluten free. The outside corridors were dirty, leave in front of our front door for our entire stay. The place looks run down and not proper maintenance. Plants growing out of the ceiling etc. Some of the staff at the restaurants did not know the menu and could help us properly.
The staff member at the desk for outdoor activities was friendly, but did not look enthusiastic. Only gave info when specifically asked about activity a or b or c etc. Did not volunteer info regarding the available activities, only referred us to the website. We missed out on many things like the JetSki, because she did not inform us. When we asked about everything available she only mentioned a few things and we assumed that that was the sum total of what was on offer. Then later we saw some other activities advertised.

The shop is poorly stocked. It is the ONLY shop on the Island and does not have ANY gluten free snacks or products or ice cream etc. There is a big opportunity to have an excellent shop, one that could be lucrative for the Island.

Me gusto: The resort itself is not bad, bedrooms renovated and fairly new facilities in the main area of the resort. But the older part of it is pretty dead like a ghost town, sports facilities pretty old and not taken care off. The snorkeling options are all enclosed in tanks rather than open in the ocean, and pretty pricey for what it is. Not proper sandy beach to swim due to the reefs. Outdoor cinema was not working.
No me gusto:
Very limited food options. I was shocked on how poor the options of food were. You stay isolated in an island with only 2 restaurants available that barely have vegetables and salads in the menu. There is not a cafe or a small grocery shop where you can purchase snacks, fruits or yogurt. 
I ordered a guacamole from the shack and the corn chips were soggy as if the bag has been opened for a while. Kids menu options are unhealthy and there is only one option of pasta for adults and kids. A vegetarian would struggle in this place to eat. I was extremely disappointed with the food options. 
The playground is quite basic and there is no much entertainment for smaller kids besides the swimming pool. 
With that being said it is pretty hard to keep toddlers entertained.

Me gusto:
A beautiful island with fantastic views from the room as well as the pool area, island beaches and some of the restaurants. The living reef was a fun addition.

Salti's restaurant was great. Good food with a nice view of the ocean and surrounding islands. Infinity restaurant food was good but I didn't like the ambience (for example, you could turn the air-conditioning down. No really, you should).


The rooms were largely clean (except the floor was not cleaned during our stay so was  not great after a couple of days). 

Loved the presence of wildlife like walaroos and cockatoos. 

Plenty to do for a three day visit. Big swimming pools, beaches, areas to snorkel, some basic watersports and easy access to tours around the Great Barrier Reef and Whitehaven beach.
No me gusto:
The hotel was a bit dated e.g. broken fixtures in the rooms, the grout needed a clean, jacuzzi out of service and filled with leaves, carpets along the corridors were filthy, some of the toilet blocks were in bad condition. Not a big deal overall - just manage your expectations. 

I would recommend opting out of the breakfast buffet. The plates were either wet, dirty or both. The buffet area was not kept clean and it took ages for the staff to attend to dirty tables. It really put me off eating so I stuck with toast! Thankfully the on site restaurants were better.

Me gusto: Such a beautiful and aesthetic resort! The room had lots of light and space. The breakfast buffet had a great variety and a lovely aquarium in the dining area. Loved seeing all the wildlife walking around the resort. Every night we were able to watch a movie as there was a really good selection and I also enjoyed the air conditioning when it was super hot.
No me gusto:
Paid extra for a ocean view room and was a little disappointed when we got a ground floor room and there was a big bush that cut off a lot of our view as-well. The bathroom vanity didn’t have a mirror on the wall you had to walk over to the other side of the bathroom to look into the mirror. Doing hair and makeup was a struggle. The cups and spoons for tea/ coffee in our room were dirty and there was no coffee provided. There are only two restaurants on the island that you really do need to book for for dinner, it is very expensive but the food was delicious. 
I would have loved to of been able to order a drink / snacks from the loungers at the pool instead of having to make my way over to the pool bar.
I was also abit disappointed when we weren’t allowed to swim in the ocean (lovers cove) unless we had stinger suits on. This came into effect on 1st November. This was not something I was aware of. There is also no room service either if you were wanting to order food to the room you can’t you have to make a take away order from the restaurant and collect it.

Me gusto:
The hotel-own aquarium was fantastic. The kids and us loved watching the different types of fish, even during breakfast. The wallabies and different birds made it special as well. 
Both restaurants offered great dishes and kid menus at reasonable prices which we enjoyed every night.
No me gusto:
It is very irritating how much the hotel and especially the rooms are worn out since the renovation/ redo a few years ago. Their photos were surely done right after renovation before opening. 
We stayed for six nights in two superior rooms with ocean view (3129 + 3127) which were both not properly cleaned. Especially under the beds we found rubbish of previous guests, sand and tons (!!!) of dust. Same in every corner of the rooms. The balconies/ terraces (ground floor) have not been cleaned before our arrival or anytime during our stay, it was covered in wallaby and bird poop. Should not be the case in a hotel such as this one. As we were traveling with young kids it made it very unpleasant.
Staff was friendly but didn't seem to care too much. Not sure if this is due to employing mostly work-and-travelers that only stay for a few weeks or months and do not work for a career in the resort. Barely seen anyone above 3o years of age. 
Thanks to the pool's life guards who collected empty glasses, cans etc. the pool area was pretty clean but it should have been the F&B colleagues instead to do so allowing the life guards to concentrate on their job. Observed this every day.
The pools were great for the kids, though the cabanas were disgusting. Reason, the wallabies sleep in them at night as the staff does not take the mattresses off. This would be so easy to do... Result, stains of wallaby poop and pee everywhere.

Me gusto: The Island is really beautiful! I recommend the rainforest walk, the views are spectacular.  The Staff were all so very kind and helpful! One evening my husband was unwell, and Tom who was taking the orders in the restaurant so kindly offered to deliver the food to our room.  All the staff were so friendly.
No me gusto: The only thing that really let the holiday down was the lack of food options.  We were there with another couple for 4 nights, and there is only a choice of 2 restaurants, so we had to alternate the two.  Unfortunately the food options are not great, and they are very over priced.  A very basic Pizza was $30.  The cost of the food was not the issue ( although had we been with our children, it would have all added up ), the main issue was the sheer lack of variety of food. The breakfast buffet was not great either. if you are travelling with kids, I highly recommend taking a big bag full of food and snacks for them, there is a good bar size fridge in the rooms.  The snacks are expensive.  Ice Cream was around $8 - $10.  if you don't like lazing by the pool, there is not much else to do at the resort, and other then the resort, there is nothing else on the Island.  when we went 10 years ago, there were more food options, and more food outlets in the resort, but for some reason, they have not been re opened.

Me gusto:
I was very pleasantly surprised at the quality of the Daydream Island Resort. 
The public areas (lobby, gym, pool areas, gardens etc) rooms and restaurants/bars are modern and stylish, and are kept in pristine condition. 
The buffet breakfast included in the room rate is very high quality with a lot of variety. 
The resort is also very generous with the free activities offered such sailing, kayaking, stand up paddle boarding, snorkelling, table tennis, pool, outdoor cinema, tennis, pool towels etc so you don’t feel you’re constantly handing money over. 
The Living Reef is very impressive, with the marine life appearing well looked after.
No me gusto:
Like many other reviews mentioned, the service at the restaurants was very disorganised, and a few times we had meals come out 30 minutes apart (and even cancelled an order as everyone else had finished) and/or could not get anyone’s attention as they all seemed to be serving all areas of the restaurant and not focusing on sections which would have helped. 
Staff could also be more disciplined about chasing the wildlife out of restaurants.

Me gusto:
Such a beautiful island in the Whitsundays! 
We had a nice time, the water was crystal clear and the weather tried to stay clear for us.
The island its self is amazing, the accommodation is improving though still has a lot of room for improvement. 
No transfers from mainland included in room packages which is a hassle and an extra cost on top. We paid over $2700 for 3 nights accommodation then another $380 for the transfer over.. 
Food on the island is nothing special, adults basic meals are around $30 up and most of the fun activities on the island cost $$. It would be great to see some big improvements to the guest experience,  they have the location, and hotel sorted, they just need a team of great staff to get it on track.

Me gusto: All the activities were great! Kayaking, trivia and music trivia and bingo, the pool was gorgeous, kids loved it and wanted to live there, the extra activies jet sking was omg the best and we did Whitehaven and snorkelling through red cat just amazing and so patient and helpful! The staff on the island we interacted with were great, especially Tom very friendly and a hard worker. All rounded great holiday. I had seen some bad reviews and was reluctant which is also why I'm posting my review because we had a great time. Although some reviews have valid points about the animals and food prices, I can understand some people may get fustrated about these things but if you know in advance you can make the decision if you think it won't annoy you or understand everyone's opinion of animal dropping everywhere can be totally different to what u think, I was expecting alot worse from the reviews then what it actually was and could just be that it is cleaner now but keep that in mind. The resort is nice, island is small but it's all to be expected if you really think about it.
No me gusto: Variety of food and not much snack variety which could have been better but to be expected on such a small island. We had the twin room for 5 of us as didn't want to pay double for extra room but was livable and fine just hard when used to your own house size it's difficult for 5 in one room lol but was to be expected and we had a great time as a family together. If your not a fussy person the resort is perfect for a great getaway with things to do if not wanting to boat to other islands. Just remember everyone's opinion about things can vary so much and you need to think about what you actually expect from a destination and if it satisfies your needs and cost, what do you value most

Me gusto:
Most of the staff at the tour desk and reception desk and breakfast were lovely. The restaurant managers were also good and tried their best. Paint and sip staff were lovely. Loved the wildlife. Great selection of plant based milks. 

Ping pong tables.were great but some backgammon or checkers in the area would be a bonus or even giant ones. 

Having pool towels was great but needing to get these early in the morning was a bit of a pain. 

Movie and TV was good for down time in the evenings. Enjoyed that it wasn't a party island.amd could get to sleep without.too.much noise. 

We were able to buy a blow up ball at the shop but there was no more pool toys.. Wasn't until we left we were told there were pool noodles to use.
No me gusto:
We were there 5 days and couldn't use any water Sports for the whole time and yet on Hamilton island with similar conditions we could use them.
The food choices for vegans were not good. The Chef told staff to charge the same price for battered fish and chips for the frozen vegan schnitzel chips and salad, ridiculous!!! The salad I ordered as a meal was bitter, something on the sauce. Two nights saw my entree and salad brought out 30mins before the kids schnitzels. A vegan dessert would be wonderful and vegan icecream especially when the weather gets warmer.

There were only 2 mugs and towels in the room despite 3 staying. No face washers in room.

We filled in the form for the room not to be cleaned and someone came in and part cleaned. Made one bed not two. Tossed out our left over pizzas (almost two full pizzas left)  in boxes for the night before which we were having for lunch later that day.. We took a couple of apples at breakfast to have later and they were gone when we got back.
Sarah at the swim bar was rude. She refused to sell me a glass of coconut water saying it was for cocktails on two occasions. One morning i asked her what time the bar closed and she told me 5pm. I turned up at 4.30 to order and she told me it was too late drinks had finished. I reminded her what she told me that morning and she said she needs time to clean up. The other lady spoke to her and said we can make one for me including a mock cocktail which Sarah had said no to. A couple of the other girls sold me a glass of coconut water when Sarah wasn't around. Pretty poor. I was told they couldn't make me a cocktail with lime as they had no line and Sarah said I could ask inside but she doubts it as there is a shortage. The inside bar said there was plenty of lime.

The restaurant staff were mixed. A couple seemed rude and only interested in doing basics and others seemed very friendly.

Me gusto: Breakfast was great. The resort was lovely, but one end of the resort was not updated and was almost derelict, assuming its going to be updated at some point. The lagoon style pools around the resort are wonderful, room was lovely, we had an ocean view room. Staff were friendly.
No me gusto: The main problem is the food. Breakfast is great, lovely buffet. However once breakfast is over if you want morning tea or afternoon tea forget it, nothing at all. There is a coffee/tea bar that is open from 7 to 12, nothing to eat though, only drinks. One place for lunch - a restaurant that serves pasta, pizza, burgers etc, no sandwiches or wraps etc. This is open from 12-3, then dinner is a choice of this restaurant again or an Asian restaurant both open from 5-9. There is also a bar, but again nothing to eat from this either. If you would like a light lunch or cake/biscuit with your morning/afternoon tea BYO from the mainland, there is nothing to eat like this here, unless you buy a pre packaged sandwich, chocolate bar or packet of crisps from the "gift shop", very limited selection, nothing Gluten free either, so if you have any dietary issues this is not ideal. There are limited lounge chairs at the Lovers Cove beach which is the little   beach part of the resort, and most of these needed repairs.

Fecha del comentario: Fecha del comentario: 4 de septiembre de 2023
Me gusto: Location is amazing, infinity restaurant was exceptional from design to food to staff and service, we were seated quickly, promptly served, food bought out quickly and cooked to perfection with the perfect amount of attention from wait staff who were happy and willing to have a joke with us. front desk and gift shop staff very welcoming. Room view and presentation was exceptional (apart from patio). Living reef clean and presented well with a beautiful variety of marine life and coral that is grown on island by thier group of wonderful and informative marine biologists. Lovers Cove pop up bar is a great idea and location for enjoying the Whitsunday sunsets. Massive improvement of public area cleanliness due to wildlife. Trivia and bingo made a great environment for all guests. Rainforest walk is exactly that, so don't expect a board walk which in itself made it spectacular with great views throughout the day but especially at sundown. Wedding chappel looks amazing and might even use it in the near future. Daydream island is definitely somewhere that we will be visiting again.
No me gusto: Wildlife access to inkstone restaurant made for an unpleasant experience due to lack of hygiene caused by droppings, animals touching table tops, staff handling Wildlife to remove from restaurant then go back to serving, also they can become aggressive. lack of table service in part to spending most of thier Time trying to remove Wildlife, staff seemed disorganised and preoccupied. Hard to get staff attention when food was delivered with no fork, Food was ok but staff didn't seem to want to be there. Patio in room was not cleaned prior to our arrival (what seemed like weeks of dust, bird droppings, spilled drinks and pastry crumbs) the most common check in request of champagne and fruit bowl upon checking in was confirmed by email then not available due to "availability" even though there is plenty of fruit and champagne on island at all times was very disappointing. South Island pool NEEDS REPAINTING, looks like something you'd expect from a seedy motel. Outdoor theatre is advertised on internet but was closed. On island Jetski tours need better advertising like a banner on the marina as they look privately owned. Usb charger on one side of bed not working. Whilst i understand the net at graze restaurant is due to guests throwing food in the living reef, surely there is a less offensive looking solution? Personal opinion is daydream Island is an amazing location with heaps of potential but needs to focus a little more on staff training in some areas, on island activities, wildlife control as the numbers seem to exceed the amount of available food (no grass, perhaps selling kangaroo feed to guests could help feed roos and allow grass to grow?) All in all the good outweighs the bad

Titulo del comentario: Great stay, staff awesome, coeliac food situation needs to improve
Tipo de Habitacion: Habitación Doble Resort con cama grande
Fecha del comentario: Fecha del comentario: 3 de agosto de 2023
Me gusto: The island itself is beautiful and every staff member we encountered was super friendly. If the weather is sunny there are protected swimming spots from most different winds. The food in both restaurants was always great tasting (but see comment below re lack of coeliac safe options). The wallaroos around the island were cute and a novelty to us.
No me gusto: The food situation. There are only two restaurants on the island, one of which is only open for dinner. The lunch/takeaway one handles the rest of the day between breakfast and dinner. For the majority of our 7 night stay, the options at this restaurant for a coeliac were inadequate and sometimes (particularly during take away periods) non-existent due to a combination of ingredients being out of stock/supply on the island or chefs on their break, so only doing even more limited a menu than the usual takeaway options, none of which were gluten free. The staff were always super friendly and tried to help but when they don't have ingredients for 5 days (eg gluten free pizza bases, things for poke bowl) it became very frustrating for all. Someone in management should have got supplies and brought them instead of waiting for the next weekly delivery to arrive and re-stock. Breakfast sometimes was also limited for coeliacs and signs were not put out for gluten free toasters etc, so people wouldn't realise and would contaminate it trying to avoid the line at the glutenful toaster. Some days hot items (eg bacon) were gluten free, others they weren't sign-posted as such so we weren't sure. I contacted management through booking halfway through our stay about this and was told it was being taken seriously and I would have a response shortly. We were there for another 4 nights after that and I'm still waiting for a response. We encountered 3 other coeliacs while we were there and all were similarly disppointed about the food situation taking away from an otherwise great holiday.  It should be made clearer when booking there's only two options to eat at on the island, and unlike Hamilton Island there's no supermarket or similar for snacks. The gift shop does sell a few bags of chips, ice creams and chocolate.

Me gusto: Daydream Island is a beautiful little island that certainly delivers in natural beauty. It is conveniently situated in the Whitsundays and easily accessibly by ferry from Airlie Beach or Hamilton Island. There is a ferry almost every hour. So this is very convenient regardless of what hour your flight arrives at either Hamilton island or Proserpine Airport. Daydream Island is also slight cheaper than Hamilton Island and very family friendly. To be honest, our first choice was Hamilton Island but we could not find any accomodation on Hamilton Island who would accept a stay of only 3 nights, except two very expensive hotels.  We could only visit for 3 nights, so what was available on Hamilton Island was too expensive. Hayman Island was even more expensive. So Daydream Island was a good second option. Also good the Cruise Whitsundays have pick ups and drop offs twice a day at/ from Daydream Island, unlike some of the other Island where you basically have to charter a boat to get there or leave. The rooms are all spacious, some have better views than others. We were a group of friends who had a total of 3 rooms. One of us had the ocean terrace view with balcony which was great. Another group also had an ocean and pool view from a different part of the hotel, and the last of the group had a pool view. The pool view was not as spectacular, but the room exactly the same size and comfort as the other two rooms. The staff were friendly, we loved the wildlife. I see some reviewers complain about the wildlife. They need to understand that the wildlife was there long before the people. It's their island and one should respect the wildlife. Live and let live ! If you don't like wildlife, go stay in a city centre! The swimming pools are great as well as the living reef and the food was fine. The Island is very family friendly, but also big enough so those who don't have children can escape to a quiet corner.
No me gusto: What I least liked about Daydream Island is the 500$ security deposit. This is quite steep for Australia. We travel a lot and there are many 5 star establishments in Sydney on the harbour with view who only dat a 100$ or 1$ deposit. This is not surprising though as the owners of Daydream Island are Asian and such large deposits are typical of places like Hong Kong and Singapore. Most of the staff were friendly but need some proper training. Some things that can be improved is making sure that guests who stay longer than 2 nights get new toilet paper, shampoo, body wash etc. We had to go ask as this was not replenished after the first night. Some reviews complain about the restaurants. We thought the food were fine , perhaps a bit expensive for what is on offer. A bigger shame was the fact that many things were not available. Even though it's an island and supplies might sometimes be an issue, with daily ferries from Hamilton Island and the Port of Airlie, one would think that at the prices they charge, they can make an effort to get enough supplies from main land Australia regularly.  There are parts of the island that still needs some renovation after cyclone Debbie. That is understandable. It would be great if those parts of the island can be updated and renovated soon, as that would add value to the resort. Perhaps also adding some more water sport options, like the motorised small boats that used to be on Daydream Island back in 2009, would be good.

Me gusto:
I recently had the pleasure of staying at the Daydream Island Resort, and I must say it was an incredible experience. From the moment I arrived, I knew I had chosen the perfect island retreat to escape from reality and leave all my worries behind.
First and foremost, the location of this resort is superb. Nestled on a picturesque island, it offers a tranquil and idyllic setting that is truly breathtaking. The moment you step foot on the island, you can feel the stress melting away, and you're instantly surrounded by the beauty of nature.
To make the most of your stay at Daydream Island Resort, a little bit of homework is necessary. However, the resort makes it easy for you by providing all the information you need. They have detailed guides on the best way to get to the island, ferry timetables, restaurant options, meal varieties, and a list of activities and locations to visit on the island. This thoughtful approach ensures that your journey is valuable and memorable.
One aspect that stood out to me was the friendliness of the staff. Despite reading some negative reviews from past guests, I found the staff to be incredibly welcoming and accommodating. They greeted us with a warm welcome upon arrival and took the time to explain everything about the resort, including meals, activities, and the best places to witness stunning sunsets. The staff's effort to maintain the resort's beauty and ensure a comfortable stay is truly commendable. They pay great attention to detail and go above and beyond to take care of their guests.
Another noteworthy feature of this resort is their excellent communication. Before our arrival, we received messages from the resort that provided all the necessary information we needed to know. This level of attention to detail ensured a smooth and hassle-free experience during our stay.
Daydream Island Resort caters to guests of all ages, making it the perfect destination for a memorable holiday.
No me gusto: While my stay at Daydream Island Resort was overall fantastic, there were a few aspects that could be improved. The cleanliness of the room was not up to par and could use some attention. Additionally, the restaurants lacked variety in their food options, and the prices were a bit high, although the food itself was undeniably delicious. Another minor inconvenience was the need to wake up early to secure a sunbed near the swimming pool; having hammocks available would have been a nice addition.

Fecha del comentario: Fecha del comentario: 27 de abril de 2026
Me gusto: It’s an island
No me gusto:
If you’ve ever dreamed of a relaxing tropical island escape… don’t wreck that dream by booking here.

We stayed just over a week, which was enough time to fully appreciate that Daydream Island appears to be running on vibes, understaffing, and pure optimism.

Let me be clear: the staff themselves were lovely. But they all looked exhausted and defeated, like contestants in the final week of a reality survival show. We didn’t meet a single employee who’d been there longer than two months, which says plenty. This feels like a management/system issue, not a staff issue.

Need a Bloody Mary? Of course, but only at the pool bar, during its mysterious and limited opening hours, so plan accordingly. Want food in the main bar or lobby area after 5pm? What an ambitious thought. Fancy a small bag of chips or soft drink from the shop? That’ll be $5 each, thanks.

The multitasking on display was honestly Olympic level. Reception staff were checking guests in while folding towels. The spa therapist was also serving in the shop. The breakfast host was greeting guests, clearing tables, wiping surfaces, and hand-drying glasses while two others appeared to be washing said glasses in buckets behind a cabinet. Buckets. On an island resort.

The buffet area itself wasn’t exactly sparkling, and dining often came with bonus entertainment from birds aggressively scouting for leftovers.

At the main lounge bar, table service is apparently a myth. Even if seated at the bar, you must queue at the register like you’re buying petrol station snacks.

We tried the “fancy” restaurant for a treat. Within two minutes of ordering, both starters and mains arrived together. When we pointed out that our $80 mains would now go cold while we ate starters, the response was a cheerful: “Sorry, the chefs are quick!” Then off they went. Efficiency without purpose is still chaos. Also, the venue itself felt less luxury dining and more conference centre with dim lighting.

Me gusto: Worst resort I have ever stayed at!
No me gusto:
for Booking.com: We stayed four nights at Daydream Island Resort and Spa after months of planning and careful research. Based on the four-star rating and the photos shown online, we expected a well-maintained island resort. Unfortunately, our experience was very different from what was advertised. We have attached photos to accurately reflect the conditions described below.

From arrival, it was clear that much of the island is poorly maintained and visibly dilapidated. There were broken fences around the tennis courts, run-down and unused buildings, and a general lack of landscaping, cleaning, and upkeep. Given how small the island is, this neglect was very noticeable and disappointing.

Our room was in poor condition, with damaged drywall and visible wear. Housekeeping was a major issue throughout our stay. We requested room cleaning three separate times. When housekeeping finally came on the third day, the service was extremely superficial — the bed was made and towels were replaced, but the bathroom was not cleaned, trash remained on the floor, and used glasses and bottles on the nightstand were not removed. The room did not appear to have been properly cleaned at all. In addition, Wi-Fi barely worked in our room, despite being advertised as an included amenity.

The common areas were also in poor condition. The laundry room had severely chipped and damaged tiles and looked very run-down. During rainy weather, multiple walkways flooded. Carpets throughout the resort were old and had a damp, mildewy smell. Soundproofing was almost nonexistent — we could clearly hear the laundry room through the walls of our room.

The dining experience raised serious concerns. Dining areas were consistently messy and appeared understaffed, with tables not being cleaned in a timely manner. We found hair in our food, were served visibly dirty dishes, and at one point received a glass of water that contained a foreign object.

Me gusto: The aquarium was a hit with the kids and the other aquatic areas too.

The facilities were poorly maintained and not very clean. A lot of staff members were sitting around on their phones instead of attending to cleaning or other tasks. There was a “games room” offered to the kids during our stay and it was very underwhelming with only a few board games on offer as well as a Nintendo game. 

The all day menu grew tired throughout our 3 night stay as it was the same offerings at all times of the day. We found it hard around lunchtime as some of our kids would nap from 12pm and no food could be ordered prior to 12pm. The food that was served at the restaurant (Salti) however was nice and good quality. The dinner buffet that we attended was not the best and left us feeling unsatisfied. The fruit selection at the breakfast buffet was not the best either which was disappointing given the tropical location. 

The room was fine however the bathroom could use some maintenance. Our shower would make an awful squealing sound! The exhaust fan did not work either and would have really been beneficial given the humidity. Some blackout curtains also wouldn’t go a stray. 

It is possible that the resort is understaffed but that being said the workers that they do have seem to be uninterested in hospitality or offering the guests an enjoyable stay. Some workers are fantastic though too!

Titulo del comentario: ⭐️ 1 Star — “Daydream Island? More like Nightmare Island.
Tipo de Habitacion: Habitación Resort con 2 camas individuales
Fecha del comentario: Fecha del comentario: 6 de noviembre de 2025
Me gusto: pool and pool beds
No me gusto:
I don’t normally leave reviews, but this stay was something else. We booked through Booking.com for a room with two queen beds — there was no option to enter guest details, but reception said it was fine. Turns out, it wasn’t. Our room keys didn’t work, and when we went back, a senior staff member literally said, “You booked through an external party, that’s not my problem.” We were then charged an extra $185.

From there, it only went downhill.

Pool & Facilities:
	•	Towels had to be collected from reception instead of being available at the pool.
	•	The pool bar was open only from 10am to 5pm, which is ridiculous for a resort.
	•	Staff were consistently rude — most acted like serving guests was an inconvenience. A simple drink took 20 minutes, and the attitude was unreal.

Food & Drinks:
	•	The salmon pasta should come with a warning label. Both myself and my travel companion were violently sick after sharing one portion.
	•	The bar staff didn’t know how to make basic drinks — I literally had to explain how to make a Malibu and Coke.
	•	They ran out of Smirnoff, refused alternatives, and wouldn’t serve a vodka soda because it was “outside spirit hours.”
	•	Saw random items like a cake and tomato sauce bottles being wheeled around on a trolley — not exactly 4-star vibes.
	•	The chocolate fondue station downstairs was sloppy and unsanitary — staff were trying to fix it with a spatula before just putting it away.

Other “Highlights”:
	•	The spa is just a hotel room converted into a “massage space.” They charged $170 for a massage — no waiver forms, no professionalism.
	•	The “latest” bar closed at 9pm.
	•	Inflatable paddle boards? Really?
	•	Wetsuits full of holes — genuinely dangerous when swimming near stingers.
	•	There was a fake evacuation and a total power outage in the foyer at one point — surreal experience.
	•	One poor staff member told us she was rostered for a double shift on her birthday.

We have been visiting Hamilton island (whitsundays) every year for the past 30 years & can not fault it whatsoever - decided to try something different this time and explore nearby Daydream island - big mistake.

property looks run down. Ants inside room. Very small rooms. Once the sun goes down there is no lighting to light up pathways & areas around pool - makes the island look deserted much like Cast Away.
Only 2 restaurants on island that are open. One tiny convenient store/souvenir store - no facilities if you want to purchase any food or necessities. Once restaurants shut you can’t even buy any snacks or drinks for your room.

We left the island after 2 nights (losing the money paid for the rest of the stay) and went to nearby Hamilton island to continue our holiday.

Me gusto: The location and the pool, that’s all….
No me gusto:
What a letdown! This resort is in desperate need of proper maintenance. The place is clearly running down — paint needs to be done inside and out, gardens completely neglected (if there are still gardeners), and our bedroom wasn’t even attended to every day.
The staff seem lost and untrained, with no idea what they’re doing. It’s honestly embarrassing for a property in such a stunning location to be left in this state.
The restaurants on the island are outrageously expensive for what they offer — and to make things worse, the food quality is downright horrifying.
Backpackers are an essential and helpful part of the resort’s workforce, but unfortunately, they appear to be left without proper supervision or guidance. The lack of experienced, steady, and professional management is evident throughout the property.
This place could thrive with the right leadership, but as it stands, the lack of structure and direction shows in every detail — from maintenance to service quality. Such a shame for a resort in such a beautiful location
It’s a real shame to see such potential wasted. I’m definitely not coming back, and honestly, I’m embarrassed to tell my friends I stayed here. How can we let a resort in such a beautiful setting be so poorly managed and abandoned?
It’s truly disappointing to see such a stunning location left in such poor condition. The resort feels abandoned — poorly maintained, neglected, and far below acceptable standards. A beautiful setting completely wasted.

There is a limited offering with restaurants at the resort and the quality of the food and service not what we expected. Overall we were disappointed with the stay and in particular the food, cleanliness and upkeep.

Titulo del comentario: Few positives don’t make up for the negatives

Fecha del comentario: Fecha del comentario: 30 de abril de 2025
Me gusto:
The grounds and pools seemed to get a lot of upkeep done throughout our stay so I will give the resort that. 

That said, the pizzas & Asian on the menu honestly were of the few things we actually enjoyed. The views were stunning, and the pools were lovely, but unfortunately, those positives didn’t make up for the rest of the experience.
No me gusto:
We stayed at Daydream Island for 3 nights as a family of 5 and were really disappointed. The food overall was BAD minus a few options below… the kitchen area in the buffet had visible mould, which was really concerning. There were crows flying around inside the buffet area — we even saw one poo on a guest, and who knows how many times they’ve done the same over the food. It felt completely unhygienic and unacceptable. Buffet definitely isn’t worth the $65 per person. 

The facilities inside the resort are extremely run down, with mould throughout multiple areas including our room. The bed sheets were dirty, towels had visible stains & blood, the shower was gross  (thankfully my kids had thongs to wear in there), and there were piles of dirty towels left in corridors. The whole place felt neglected and poorly.

If you’re unsure about visiting this place, like we were as we saw a lot of mixed reviews, let this review be the one that stops you. I’m coming from a totally honest place, this was a very disappointing stay and absolutely not worth the money, we will not be back nor recommending this resort to anyone.

Me gusto: The cocktails were good, the food and staff at infinity were amazing, the view is quite good.
No me gusto: The hotel is very dirty and not taken care of well. The hotel is not entirely enclosed and the carpet is disgustingly and needs a vacuum. The first room i was allocated had a poorly made bed with clearly dirty sheets, mismatched pillows, mould over the furniture and the TV and Phone were broken. The staff then provided me a new room which was better quality but there was dried food on the wall near the bin and ants were coming in as a result. Housekeeping only came at 8am which isn’t ideal for solo travellers who would prefer a small sleep in on holiday. A major problem is the poor wifi strength , given all menus, maps and information about your stay are electronic, i could rarely access this information and when i asked the staff if the quality was poor or if there was an issue they were unhelpful. Through research of my own, certain network providers only have 4g coverage in this area so you will need to manually change your phones default setting from 5g to access the internet due to the spotty wifi. I do not recommend this island for solo travellers or couples, it’s geared toward children and families and was not a positive experience. I suggest spending a little more money and staying on a nicer island.

Me gusto: Beautiful location with fantastic wildlife
No me gusto:
Overall a really disappointing stay. An anniversary trip that was ignored, despite m emailing about this prior to our stay and mentioning to staff at our anniversary dinner. 
Initial room was dirty and had not been cleaned, and the second had an ant infestation (a few can obviously be ignored as you’re in the tropics but they covered every surface).
Dining was fine, with tasty food but poor service dampened the experience - items ordered frequently missed and long waits despite half empty restaurants; the crow that dominated the breakfast area meaning you were unable to leave anything unattended without it being swiped by the resident bird was certainly an interesting one.
Staff were very helpful when an issue was raised, it just became increasingly disappointing with the number of issues that continue to arise.
Selling it’s inclusive activities being sea kayaking and sailing but only being allowed to take these 100m out to sea and across a 250m stretch of sea - to call this an activity would be an overstatement in what you were able to do. 
A resort that relies on the beauty of its surroundings rather than the quality of the resort and the service provided. Overall incredibly disappointed.

Me gusto: Daydream Island is amazing, we thoroughly enjoyed our stay on Daydream Island. The views were sensational. The food was a little bit expensive but it was very good food. The staff were amazing, polite and friendly. Our holiday was short however it was very relaxing. We will be returning to Daydream Island with our children.
No me gusto:
The bed was so soft they had no springs in the mattress making our sleep very uncomfortable. The house keeping was not as good as the price we paid for a Ocean view room. Cleaning needs more attention. Also the laundry room particularly the washing machines need to be cleaned they had a smell that was not nice. 

Upon checking out we had an issue were we paid our room bill the day before check out. Without any knowledge the payment was showing it had declined in their system. The manager at the time insisted we need to pay the bill again and the payment we made the night before will bounce back into our account. It had declined and could be because of a connection error. We paid the room bill with another card. The lady working the front desk who was servicing us was polite, friendly and professional but the manager at that time was not professional. As we were unable to identify the person working the front desk the night prior to our check out. The manager was pushy and was firm that he was unsure of who we dealt with because I questioned him that he should know who works the front desk. He continued to proceed with questioning us with who we spoke to, because he didnt get an answer he just walked away. Daydream Island has cameras and a roster for a reason, please do your own investigations before being pushy with customer. As he stated he had a lot of staff, a customer may not remember your staff either. However this is well noted for next time. 

We left Daydream Island on 25/11/24, Thursday 28/11/24 the payment we made the night before checkout went through and CLEARED, it DID NOT bounce back into our account like the Manager had stated would happen. This I currently being followed up with the resort. 

Please take a hard copy of the receipt, proof of payments  will be required if this happens to you. 

Dispute the payment problem and communication barrier from the Manager. Daydream Island is a beautiful place.
